channel.title: Whalesbook Financial News
channel.description: Latest Indian and global financial market news

item.title: Lodha Targets Rs 24,000 Crore Sales, Up 17%, Amid Housing Slowdown
item.description: Macrotech Developers, also known as Lodha, has set an ambitious 17% growth target for sales bookings, aiming for Rs 24,000 crore in fiscal year 2026-27. This follows a strong 16% increase to Rs 20,530 crore in FY26, which also saw a record net profit of Rs 3,430.7 crore on total income of Rs 17,119.5 crore. The company is pushing forward despite a general slowdown in the Indian housing market since 2025, relying on its strong brand and financial capabilities to focus on profitable growth with low debt.

item.title: Adani Energy Solutions Plans ₹22,000 Cr Spending Surge for FY27
item.description: Adani Energy Solutions (AESL) is sharply increasing its capital expenditure to ₹22,000 crore for FY27, a 47% jump from current levels. The majority of this investment will focus on the transmission sector, with ₹15,500 crore allocated. Distribution will receive ₹2,350 crore, and smart metering initiatives ₹3,900 crore. This ambitious spending plan continues into FY28 with projected outlays of ₹25,000 crore, mainly for transmission infrastructure. AESL also plans to accelerate its smart meter rollout, targeting one crore installations this fiscal year, building on recent successes like the Mumbai HVDC project.

item.title: ED Raids Raheja Developers Amid Fraud Claims, Hitting Buyer Trust
item.description: The Enforcement Directorate (ED) conducted fresh searches across approximately seven locations in Delhi-NCR on Saturday, targeting Raheja Developers Limited. This action stems from an ongoing money laundering investigation linked to allegations of widespread fraud against home buyers and the failure to deliver units in the Raheja Revanta project. The intensified scrutiny comes as the company faces existing insolvency proceedings and a market increasingly wary of developer accountability.

item.title: Paytm Shifts Focus to High-Margin Services After RBI Bans Payments Bank
item.description: Paytm (One 97 Communications Ltd) has assured investors there's no direct financial impact after the Reserve Bank of India canceled its associate Paytm Payments Bank's license. The company highlighted its prior investment impairment and PPBL's independent operations. The move prompts Paytm to sharpen its focus on higher-margin financial services and merchant solutions, businesses already showing strong profits.

item.title: India Pesticide Bill Debate: Industry Warns of Stifled Innovation
item.description: India plans a new Pesticide Management Bill to update rules from 1968. While the government wants to boost industry and protect farmers, the draft faces major criticism. Industry groups worry about weak R&D data protection, unclear e-commerce rules, and the Central Pesticides Board's limited advisory power, fearing it could block innovation and add compliance costs to a growing market.

item.title: FIIs Dump ₹17,140 Cr Equities; DIIs Buy ₹9,780 Cr Amid Market Slump
item.description: Indian equity markets closed the week lower, with the Sensex and Nifty 50 declining nearly 2% due to significant foreign institutional investor (FII) outflows. FIIs sold ₹17,140 crore in equities, while domestic institutional investors (DIIs) bought ₹9,780 crore, partially cushioning the impact. Geopolitical tensions in the Middle East and weakness in IT stocks drove the sentiment.

item.title: US Sanctions Hengli Petrochemical Over Iran Oil Purchases
item.description: The US Treasury has sanctioned Hengli Petrochemical for allegedly buying billions in Iranian oil. The move targets a major Chinese independent refiner, often called a "teapot," and its shipping network. Beijing protested the sanctions, calling them a geopolitical tool and accusing the US of politicizing trade. The action highlights China's key role in Iran's oil exports, even under US pressure. Hengli Petrochemical trades at a higher valuation than state-owned rivals amid this tension.

item.title: Paytm Payments Bank License Revoked by RBI Over Compliance Failures
item.description: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has cancelled Paytm Payments Bank's license, citing serious compliance failures that harmed depositors and the public. This action stops all banking operations for Paytm Payments Bank, formalizing earlier restrictions. While parent company One97 Communications will continue its payment services through partner banks, this regulatory move presents a major challenge and requires significant strategic shifts.

item.title: India Motor Insurance: High Commissions Fuel Mis-selling, Hurting Buyers
item.description: India's motor insurance market, expected to reach over $10 billion by 2026, struggles with widespread mis-selling. Sales agents, driven by high upfront commissions, often push policies based on suitability. This results in hidden policy exclusions, altered Insured Declared Values (IDVs), and unnecessary bundled extras, leading to many customer complaints. Despite regulator action, complaints about "unfair business practices" increased by 14% in FY25, showing ongoing problems that harm customer trust and hide the sector's real growth.

item.title: SpaceX $75B IPO to Drain Crypto, Tech Liquidity Pools
item.description: The market faces a potential liquidity crunch as blockbuster IPOs from SpaceX, OpenAI, and Anthropic are slated for the latter half of the year. These behemoth listings could collectively draw over $240 billion from investors, impacting capital available for speculative assets like cryptocurrencies and technology equities.

item.title: AAI Engineers Warn ATC Privatisation Threatens National Security
item.description: Engineers from Airports Authority of India's ATSEPA have alerted the Civil Aviation Minister to risks in proposed air traffic control (ATC) privatisation. They argue proceeding without an independent Air Navigation Services (ANS) entity could harm India's national strategic capabilities and operational integrity.

item.title: Brokerages Pick Top 10 'Buy' Stocks with Up to 48% Upside
item.description: Indian markets dipped 2% this week, but top research firms are bullish on ten select stocks. These 'Buy' rated equities offer potential gains of 15% to 48%, driven by strong fundamentals and growth prospects across various sectors.

item.title: TCS, Wipro Hike Salaries for AI Talent; Infosys's Raises Delayed
item.description: India's top IT firms are signaling distinct market outlooks through their salary hike policies. TCS and Wipro are giving annual raises to keep talent amid fierce AI skill competition. Infosys's cautious, delayed approach suggests a more conservative view on future demand or cost control. This difference highlights varying management confidence and strategy in the fast-changing tech sector.

item.title: Global PMI Rebound Masks Stress as AI Divides Markets
item.description: Global private sector activity shows a rebound, but this is driven by stockpiling rather than genuine demand, signaling deferred stress. The AI revolution strongly benefits South Korea and Taiwan, while India faces potential inflation and disruption in its IT services sector. This creates an 'unstable equilibrium' for investors.

item.title: NSE IPO: Shareholder EOI Deadline Approaches Amid Strict Rules
item.description: The National Stock Exchange (NSE) IPO is moving forward, with existing shareholders facing a strict April 27, 2026 deadline to submit Expressions of Interest (EOI). Key restrictions include sellers not being able to re-invest and shares needing to be held since June 15, 2025. This process occurs as the unlisted market valuation reaches nearly ₹4.7 lakh crore, while market sentiment increasingly favors profitability, creating challenges for the offering.

item.title: India IT Sector: Can AI Hedge Strategy Survive AI Disruption?
item.description: Mirae Asset Mutual Fund CEO Swarup Mohanty suggests India's IT sector could be a 'non-AI hedge' for global portfolios as AI investment peaks. However, this view faces challenges. Generative AI is fundamentally changing the IT services landscape. While India's economy offers growth, IT firms risk becoming price-takers in the AI era, hampered by lower R&D and a limited share in high-value AI segments. Adaptation is key for survival, not just hedging, against fierce competition and client demands for AI-native solutions.

item.title: US Chip Curbs Fuel China's Race for Domestic Tech
item.description: New U.S. legislation aims to restrict advanced semiconductor technology exports to China. In response, Beijing is accelerating its push for domestic chip self-sufficiency, investing heavily in local manufacturing and design. This tension arises as global demand for chips, especially for AI, remains strong, but it carries significant risks of breaking apart supply chains and creating separate tech markets.

item.title: Mastek Sells Chennai Property for ₹60 Cr to Caresoft Mobility
item.description: Mastek Ltd. has agreed to sell its Chennai commercial building for ₹60 crore to Caresoft Mobility Pvt Ltd. This move to monetize physical assets supports Mastek's focus on digital and AI solutions. However, the buyer's newness and low capital raise questions about the deal's finalization.

item.title: India-US Energy Pact Grows: Coal & LNG Deals Deepen Partnership
item.description: India's Ambassador to the US, Vinay Mohan Kwatra, met with US Energy Secretary Chris Wright to strengthen their energy partnership. They discussed civil nuclear power, coal gasification, and US liquefied petroleum gas (LNG) exports. This follows recent steps to boost energy trade, including major US coal shipments and a GAIL LNG vessel dispatch.

item.title: India Inc. Q4 Earnings: Key Firms to Reveal Economic Health
item.description: India's corporate earnings season begins this week with over 180 companies, including Coal India, Maruti Suzuki, and Hindustan Unilever, releasing their Q4 FY26 results. The market, already shaky from geopolitical tensions and IT sector weakness, will closely examine these results for insights into the economy's health, margin trends, and future outlooks. Banking sector performance is a key focus amid deposit cost pressures, while commodity firms face external demand uncertainties.

item.title: Pronto Valuation Skyrockets to $200M Amid Investor Rush
item.description: Instant house-help startup Pronto is finalizing a $20 million funding round led by tech investor Lachy Groom, catapulting its valuation to $200 million post-investment. This marks a rapid doubling of its valuation from just weeks prior, underscoring intense investor focus on the burgeoning Indian home services market. The company's aggressive expansion and significant daily order volume growth are key drivers behind this accelerated funding trajectory.

item.title: Haryana Sacks Superintendent in ₹590 Cr IDFC First Bank Fraud
item.description: Haryana has dismissed Naresh Bhuwani, a Superintendent in the Development and Panchayat Department, for alleged involvement in a ₹590 crore fraud linked to IDFC First Bank. The government states substantial evidence points to a criminal conspiracy diverting government funds to shell companies, with Bhuwani accused of siphoning money for personal assets.

item.title: Horse Powertrain Eyes India: Hybrid Bet Amidst EV Transition
item.description: Engine maker Horse Powertrain is setting up a legal entity in India, anticipating a Q3 resolution, to tap into the market's demand for hybrid and efficient internal combustion engines (ICE). CEO Matias Giannini highlighted India's potential not just as a sales market but as a future development and export hub. This move capitalizes on India's regulatory shifts, favorable tax structures for small vehicles, and a broader industry trend that acknowledges the enduring role of hybrid and ICE technologies alongside electrification.

item.title: India & Russia RELOS Deal Goes Live, Reshaping Defense Buys
item.description: The India-Russia Reciprocal Exchange of Logistics (RELOS) agreement is now operational, allowing each country to host significant military assets from the other. The pact permits stationing up to 3,000 troops, five warships, and ten aircraft. This marks a strategic step for India to boost its defense procurement diversification and strengthen supply chains amid global pressures and a shifting arms market.

item.title: Groww Overtakes Zerodha: Profit Jumps 142% on 62% Margins
item.description: Fintech giant Groww has overtaken Zerodha to become India's largest retail broker by active clients. In Q4 FY26, revenue soared 88% to ₹1,505 crore, with operating profit jumping 142% to ₹938 crore. Margins hit a strong 62%, showing how its scale boosts profits and focuses on getting more value from users, not just signing them up.

item.title: Persistent Systems Stock Dives 5% Despite 24 Quarters of Growth
item.description: Persistent Systems reported strong Q4 FY26 results, with revenue up 25.1% year-on-year in rupees and profit after tax jumping 33.7%. This marked the company's 24th straight quarter of sequential revenue growth. However, shares dropped 5.3% as the market reacted to growth rates that, while robust, did not significantly exceed already high expectations.

item.title: Tata Elxsi Attracts FIIs Betting on AI Amid IT Sector Downturn
item.description: Foreign investors bought more shares of Tata Elxsi in Q4 FY26, even as other Indian IT firms were sold off. Tata Elxsi's focus on AI and design services attracted this interest. While its recent quarterly results were strong, the company's full-year revenue growth was small and profit fell, raising questions about its high stock valuation in today's market.

item.title: Triveni Engineering Splits Power Unit, Eyes Major Valuation Jump
item.description: Triveni Engineering & Industries (TEIL) is spinning off its Power Transmission Business (PTB) into a new listed entity, Triveni Power Transmission Limited (TPTL). This strategic move aims to separate TEIL's strong, profitable engineering division from its cyclical sugar and alcohol businesses. The goal is to unlock significant value for shareholders by avoiding the lower valuation common for diversified companies and attracting investors focused on TPTL's specific growth potential. Initial estimates suggest the new power company could be valued much higher than its current contribution to TEIL.

item.title: Keystone Realtors' Redevelopment Bet: Sales Soar, Profits Trail
item.description: Keystone Realtors Ltd is strategically shifting from traditional land acquisition to a redevelopment-focused model in Mumbai. While the company demonstrates robust operational momentum with a 23% year-over-year increase in pre-sales and significant project pipeline growth, the conversion of these sales into net profits remains a key challenge, with a net profit of ₹5 crore on ₹266 crore revenue in Q3 FY26. Its strong balance sheet and low debt (0.22x Gross Debt/Equity) provide crucial "time" to navigate the execution cycle, positioning the company for a potential market re-rating once financial outcomes align with operational scale.

item.title: Musk Drops Most Claims vs. OpenAI, Valuation Soars Amid AI Race
item.description: OpenAI secured a significant win as Elon Musk dropped most fraud claims, streamlining the high-profile lawsuit just before trial. This legal recalibration arrives as OpenAI commands an $852 billion valuation following a massive $122 billion funding round in March 2026. Despite substantial revenue growth, the company projects $14 billion in losses for 2026, highlighting a critical profitability gap amid intense competition from rivals like Anthropic and Elon Musk's xAI.

item.title: Nippon India, Mirae Asset Split Stock Buys: Focus vs. Diversify
item.description: In March 2026, Nippon India Mutual Fund and Mirae Asset Mutual Fund bought equities with different strategies. Mirae Asset diversified, adding 54 stocks, while Nippon India concentrated on 28 high-conviction picks. Both favored consumer discretionary, financial services, and mid-cap stocks.

item.title: HDFC Bank: 97% Digital Transactions, Strong Branch Network Fuels Growth
item.description: HDFC Bank is pursuing a dual strategy, handling 97% of payments digitally while keeping its nearly 10,000 branches active. This approach aims to capture broad customer needs and drive growth in deposits, MSME, and mortgages, while boosting operating leverage. Investor confidence is reflected in its P/E ratio of about 15.8-17.4.

item.title: CFTC Battles States Over Prediction Markets, Impacting Bullish (BLSH)
item.description: The U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) is aggressively asserting exclusive federal jurisdiction over prediction markets, suing states like New York to block their attempts to regulate these platforms as illegal gambling. This escalating legal battle, with numerous states backing the regulatory push, creates significant uncertainty for emerging financial instruments and associated companies such as Bullish Inc. (BLSH), whose valuation metrics are under scrutiny amid market volatility and revised earnings forecasts.

item.title: Nifty 50 Faces 24,250 Resistance; SBI Analyst Picks PNB Housing, Glenmark for Gains
item.description: Nifty 50 is facing resistance near 24,200-24,250 and support at 23,700. SBI Securities analyst Sudeep Shah sees potential for bullish momentum if Nifty breaks higher. He recommends PNB Housing Finance and Glenmark Pharma as top midcap stock picks for the coming week, citing strong technical indicators for both.

item.title: Nvidia Hits $5 Trillion as AI Leader, Faces New Rivals and Scrutiny
item.description: Nvidia hit a record $5 trillion market cap on April 24, 2026, with its stock reaching $208.26, fueled by AI chip demand. The company now faces growing competition, increased regulatory scrutiny, and major clients developing their own chips, posing challenges to its market leadership.

item.title: Pramodini Medicare IPO: ₹45 Crore Raise to Fuel Diagnostics Expansion
item.description: Andhra Pradesh-based Pramodini Medicare is seeking to raise ₹45.1 crore through an Initial Public Offering (IPO) to purchase medical equipment and fund future acquisitions. The diagnostic service provider operates 15 centres across six states and employs a diversified business model involving public-private partnerships. Despite robust profit growth, the company faces a fragmented market dominated by larger players like Krsnaa Diagnostics, necessitating careful execution of its expansion strategy.

item.title: India's Fiscal Tightrope: Freebies Fuel Consumption, Strain Budgets
item.description: India's economy faces a fiscal challenge as government cash transfers and 'freebies' boost consumption, especially in rural areas and for FMCG companies. However, these measures significantly increase state and central government debt and deficits, raising concerns about long-term sustainability and diverting funds from productive investment. Rising bond yields amid energy price shocks add to fiscal pressure.
